name: Standing Band Leg Curl
resistance_profile: Band tension rises through the curl and peaks at full knee flexion — a shortened-biased knee-flexion movement that complements stretch-loaded hamstring work.
cues: Keep the thigh still — only the knee moves
Squeeze the hamstring hard at the top
Control the band back down, don't let it snap you
execution_steps: Anchor one end of a loop band to a low point (a rack upright or a closed door) and loop the other end around your working ankle.
Stand facing the anchor holding a rack or wall for balance, with light tension already on the band and the working foot slightly forward.
Keeping your thigh vertical and hips still, curl your heel up toward your glute against the band.
Squeeze at the top, then lower under control to full knee extension and repeat before switching legs.
good_when: Training at home or travelling with only bands
You want contracted-position hamstring work to complement RDLs
You want high-rep pump or rehab work that is easy on the joints
bad_when: You need heavy, precise progressive overload
You have a leg-curl machine and want maximal loading
You want a lengthened-biased stimulus (the band is lightest at the stretch)
common_errors: Swinging the hip to help curl the weight up
Letting the band yank the leg back down
Standing too close so there is no tension at the bottom
progressions: Thicker or shorter band for more tension
Add a pause and a slow eccentric at peak contraction
regressions: Reduce the range to a comfortable, pain-free arc
